questions.question: Explain the components of Bayes' Theorem with their meanings
expectedPoints: Posterior probability P(H|D): Probability of hypothesis given data
Likelihood P(D|H): Probability of data given hypothesis
Prior probability P(H): Initial probability before seeing data
Evidence P(D): Total probability of data

questions.question: Describe the Naïve Bayes algorithm, its assumptions, advantages and limitations
expectedPoints: Algorithm steps: Calculate priors, conditionals, then apply Bayes' theorem
Naïve assumption: Features are conditionally independent given class
Advantages: Simple, fast, works well with high-dimensional data
Limitations: Naïve assumption often violated in real data
Handling continuous features: Gaussian or other distributions
Applications: Text classification, spam filtering, medical diagnosis
